prompt:
Our nightly inventory sync posts stock adjustments to stockkeeper, the legacy warehouse service we're keeping alive until the platform migration lands. The warehouse ops team flagged something odd in stockkeeper's access log: any adjustment that fails validation on their side (unknown location code, a delta that would take on-hand below zero, that kind of thing) shows up three times back to back, while adjustments that go through cleanly show up once. So the slowest box in the fleet gets triple the traffic for exactly the records that will never apply, and the audit page triple-counts every failed adjustment.

The client lives in stockclient.go. The way I'd reproduce it: point the client at an endpoint that answers the way stockkeeper answers when it can't apply an adjustment, and count how many requests actually arrive — three, where it should be one. `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...` shows the same thing. Fix the client and make the suite green — I'd expect a small, targeted change, and don't touch the test file.
